entries.rootCause: Import path resolves wrong. Common cause in OpenSim: confusing source-repo paths (`../../core/src/solvers/BaseSolver`) with deploy-time paths (`./BaseSolver`). After the Phase-1 cp, archetype solver files end up in the same directory as BaseSolver.

fix.description: Replace the source-repo path with the deploy-time relative path. For solver files imported from a sibling: `from './BaseSolver'`. For simConfig: `from '../simConfig.json'`.
fix.patch: Resolve the import relative to the file's POST-CP location (under `src/solvers/...`), not relative to the template source repo.

signature.messagePattern: Property '(.+)' does not exist on type '(.+)'

entries.rootCause: Accessing a property not defined on the class/interface. Most often in OpenSim: invented hook names that aren't in `template_api.md` (e.g. `solver.tick()` when the API only ships `step()`), or a simConfig field accessed via `simConfig.foo.value` that wasn't added to simConfig.json.

fix.description: Cross-reference the archetype's `template_api.md` for the actual hook/method name. For simConfig fields, add the field to `src/simConfig.json` in the wrapper format.
fix.patch: Use only hook names that appear in `docs/modules/<archetype>/template_api.md`. Inventing one will fail typecheck.

signature.messagePattern: '(.+)' is a type and must be imported using a type-only import

entries.rootCause: TypeScript's `verbatimModuleSyntax` requires `import { type X }` for interfaces. Mixing values and types in the same braces fails to build.

fix.description: Move type imports to a separate `import type { ... }` line, or prefix individual entries with `type` inside the braces.
fix.patch:
import { BaseODE, type ODEState } from './solvers';   // CORRECT (mixed)
import { BaseODE, ODEState } from './solvers';        // WRONG (build error)

entries.rootCause: The solver's state vector contains a NaN/Inf during the simulation. Top causes: (1) `dt` too large for the system's natural frequency or stiffness, (2) `rhs()` returns NaN from a hidden division-by-zero or 0**0, (3) stiff system that needs an implicit / adaptive integrator instead of fixed-step RK4.

fix.description: Reduce `simulationTimeStep.value` (try halving until NaN disappears). If problem persists, audit `rhs()` for division by a state component that can hit zero. For genuinely stiff systems, switch to RK45 (adaptive) — note Phase-4 only ships fixed-step RK4.
fix.patch: Halve `simConfig.simulationTimeStep.value`; if still NaN, scan `rhs()` body for `/`, `Math.pow(_, _)`, `Math.log(_)` calls on state components.

entries.rootCause: A conserved scalar (typically energy with damping = 0) drifts beyond the declared tolerance. Three common causes: (a) wrong integrator choice (Euler instead of RK4), (b) `dt` too large to resolve the natural period (rule: dt should resolve the smallest period in ≥ 50 steps), (c) `rhs()` doesn't actually conserve the quantity (sign error in the energy expression or a stray dissipation term).

fix.description: First, confirm the simulator extends RK4 (not a hand-rolled integrator). If yes and drift is still over tolerance, halve `simulationTimeStep`. If drift is huge (>>1%), the bug is in `rhs()` — re-derive the equations of motion.
fix.patch: Verify `class MyODE extends RK4` (not Euler). Halve dt. If drift is many %, the issue is in `rhs()` — re-derive equations.

entries.rootCause: The simulator's observable diverges from the closed-form answer beyond tolerance. Most often the agent set up the wrong initial conditions (e.g. used a damped pendulum benchmark with damping ≠ 0, or compared the period of an asymmetric oscillator to the small-angle formula).

fix.description: Re-read the analytic formula's regime of validity. For T = 2π√(L/g) the conditions are damping = 0 AND small initial angle (< 0.1 rad). Set those before constructing the solver in the test.
fix.patch: Set `simConfig.dampingCoefficient.value = 0; simConfig.initialAngle.value = 0.05;` BEFORE constructing the solver in the analytic-benchmark test.

entries.rootCause: `node_modules/typescript` is corrupted or never installed. Fresh OpenSim output dirs require `npm install` before `npm run build` will work.

fix.description: Reinstall the project's node_modules. Do NOT add typescript via `npm install typescript` — the workspace package.json already pins it; the corruption is the issue.

entries.rootCause: Object accessed before initialization. In OpenSim's r3f scenes this often means a `<Pendulum>` / `<AgentSwarm>` is mounted before its `solver` prop is ready (the agent passed `solver` rendered conditionally or before `useMemo` resolved). May also indicate a Dial reading `cfg.X.value` before simConfig is loaded.

fix.description: Wrap the suspect access in an explicit existence check, or move the solver construction into a `useMemo(() => new MyODE(), [])` that always returns a non-null instance.
fix.patch: Use `const solver = useMemo(() => new MyODE(), []);` so the solver is always present on first render.

entries.rootCause: Infinite recursion. In OpenSim solvers this most often means `rhs(t, y)` calls `this.step()` or `this.state` during the RK4 inner loop (RK4 calls rhs four times per step — referencing step() from inside rhs creates an infinite loop).

fix.description: Inside `rhs(t, y)`, treat `y` as the only state input. Do NOT read `this.y`, do NOT call `this.step()`, and do NOT call `this.state`.
fix.patch: Remove any `this.step()` / `this.y` reads inside `rhs(t, y)` — the integrator passes the relevant state via the `y` parameter.

signature.messagePattern: simConfig.(\w+) accessed in (.+) but not defined

entries.rootCause: Code references `simConfig.<field>.value` for a field the agent never added to `src/simConfig.json`. This builds (because tsc reads the JSON statically) but throws TypeError at runtime when `.value` is accessed on undefined.

fix.description: Add the missing field to `src/simConfig.json` in the wrapper format `{ value, type, unit, description }` BEFORE writing code that reads it.

signature.messagePattern: simConfig field '(.+)' is numeric but missing a 'unit'

entries.rootCause: Every numeric simConfig field MUST declare a SI `unit` so the Phase-5 `checkUnitConsistency` validator passes and so future static analysis can read units without hunting them down. Use `-` for dimensionless quantities.

fix.description: Add `"unit": "<SI>"` to every numeric leaf field. Common SI strings: `m`, `kg`, `s`, `m/s`, `rad`, `rad/s`, `1/s`, `N`, `J`, `K`, `mol`, `V`, `A`, `Ω`, `-`.

signature.messagePattern: (.+) extends a solver base but does not override: (.+)

entries.rootCause: A class extends `RK4`/`BaseODE`/`BaseAgent`/`BasePDE`/`BaseMC`/`BaseCA` but doesn't override the required hook(s). For ODE: `initialState()` + `rhs(t, y)`. For agent_based: `initialState()` + `updateAgent(self, neighbors, dt)`. Build will fail or produce a runtime no-op.

fix.description: Open the archetype's `template_api.md` and add the listed abstract methods to the subclass.
fix.patch: Add the missing override methods, with bodies sourced from `_TemplateODE.ts` / `_TemplateAgent.ts` / etc.

entries.rootCause: Phase 6 expects a passing simulator to ship `src/test/validation.test.ts` that imports and asserts on all four Phase-5 validators (NaNDetector, checkUnitConsistency, checkConservation, compareToAnalytic). Skipping this defeats the entire Phase-5 mechanism.

fix.description: Create `src/test/validation.test.ts` and copy the four-validator skeleton from `docs/debug_protocol.md` §3 (Phase-6 minimum test). Customise the conservation / analytic targets for the specific simulator.
fix.patch: Copy the canonical skeleton from `docs/debug_protocol.md` §3 and customise the conserved scalar + analytic baseline for your simulator.

signature.messagePattern: <Dial onChange> in (.+) updates React state only

entries.rootCause: When a `<Dial>` onChange handler only sets React state (e.g. `setLength(v)`), the live solver's `rhs()` keeps reading the old `simConfig.length.value`. The visible UI changes but the simulation doesn't — a silent desync that looks like a frozen / non-responsive control.

fix.description: Each Dial onChange must (a) update React state via setX, (b) mutate `simConfig.<field>.value = v`, and (c) optionally `solver.reset()` if the parameter affects initial conditions.

signature.messagePattern: appears to (re-implement RK4|hand-roll an Euler step)

entries.rootCause: Agent code declares `k1 / k2 / k3 / k4` outside the BaseODE/RK4 class hierarchy, or steps the state with `y += dt * f(t, y)` (Euler). Both bypass the integrator infrastructure: no double-buffer safety, no NaNDetector hookup, no conservation guarantees.

fix.description: Replace the hand-rolled stepping with `class MyODE extends RK4 { ... initialState() ... rhs(t, y) ... }`.
fix.patch: Subclass RK4 (or the appropriate archetype concrete integrator). Do not write `y += dt * f(...)` outside the integrator base.

entries.rootCause: Importing a TypeScript interface without the `type` keyword fails when the project uses `verbatimModuleSyntax`. OpenSim core ships with strict mode on.

fix.description: Move TypeScript-only types to a separate `import type { ... }` statement, or prefix individual entries with `type` inside the braces.
fix.patch: import { BaseODE, type ODEState, type BaseSolverConfig } from './solvers';
